message:
test: add 13 unit tests for extension_tools + fix session_sqlite formatting

- extension_tools.rs: 13 new tests covering constructor defaults, timeout
  clamping, ctx_payload builder, collect_wrappers (empty/multi/filter),
  JS error mapping, empty content, isError flag, and input passthrough
- session_sqlite.rs: formatting fixes from cargo fmt

Total lib tests: 2572

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1bje","title":"Implement mock layer for Rust QuickJS runtime","description":"# Implement mock layer for Rust QuickJS runtime

## Context
The Rust runtime executes extensions in QuickJS. The hostcall bridge (src/extensions.rs dispatch_hostcall_events/dispatch_hostcall_session) handles JS->Rust calls. For conformance testing, we need to intercept these and return mock responses from the shared mock spec.

## Architecture
In Rust, hostcalls flow:
1. JS calls pi.session(op, payload) or pi.events(op, payload)
2. QuickJS calls back to Rust via registered hostcall functions
3. Rust dispatch_hostcall_events() / dispatch_hostcall_session() routes to real implementations
4. For mocking: we need TestSession / TestDispatcher that reads mock spec

## What Already Exists
- NullSession in extension_dispatcher.rs: returns empty/default for all session calls
- TestSession in extension_dispatcher.rs: similar but for tests
- These are close to what we need but lack mock spec integration

## What To Do
1. Create ConformanceMockSession that implements ExtensionSession trait
2. ConformanceMockSession reads mock_spec.json and returns configured responses
3. Create ConformanceMockDispatcher for non-session hostcalls (exec, http, etc.)
4. Create a test function: load extension + run with mocks + capture output as JSON
5. Output format must EXACTLY match the TS harness output format

## Key Implementation Details
- JsExtensionSnapshot already captures registrations (tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers)
- We need to also capture hostcall invocations during load
- The mock layer goes between the extension and the real dispatch

## Acceptance Criteria
- ConformanceMockSession passes compilation
- Load hello.ts extension with mock session, capture registrations
- Output JSON matches TS harness format","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:30.492512632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:00.036719300Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:00.036620516Z","close_reason":"Created tests/conformance_mock.rs with 11 tests implementing the conformance mock layer: ConformanceMockSpec (JSON-loadable spec), ConformanceMockSession (implements ExtensionSession with capture logging), HostcallCaptureLog (records all hostcall invocations), ConformanceOutput/ConformanceRegistrations (structured output format for diff-based conformance testing). Tests cover: mock session compilation + default spec, configured state returns, mutation capture (set_name/set_model/set_thinking_level/set_label), spec JSON deserialization, capture log serialization, hello.ts loading with mock session, tool registration capture, session-calling extension loading, multi-registration type capture (commands+shortcuts+flags+providers+models), and JSON round-trip. All tests pass, clippy clean (lib error is pre-existing in interactive.rs), fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bje","depends_on_id":"bd-1e1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:30.522833776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bje","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:30.509182032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1bzn","title":"Session picker: delete sessions (Ctrl+D) using trash when available","description":"# Goal
Add legacy session deletion UX to the session picker.

# Legacy Spec
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/session.md`:
- In `/resume`, select a session and press `Ctrl+D`, then confirm.
- If `trash` CLI is available, use it to avoid permanent deletion.

# Required Behavior
- While in session picker:
  - `Ctrl+D` triggers a confirmation dialog.
  - On confirm:
    - prefer invoking `trash <file>` if `trash` is in PATH.
    - else delete the JSONL file directly (and remove from index).
  - Update the picker list immediately.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep deletion *non-destructive by default* by preferring trash.
- Provide clear feedback on failure.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session delete works and updates the picker list.
- [ ] Uses `trash` when available.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:24.524917111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:49.115866554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:51:59.095123066Z","close_reason":"Implemented session picker delete with Ctrl+D: added confirm_delete state to SessionPickerOverlay, delete confirmation flow (y/n/Esc), delete_session_file() method that uses trash CLI when available with fallback to direct removal, and updated help text to show Ctrl+D hint.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bzn","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bzn","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1cd5","title":"Settings: persist changes to ~/.pi/agent/settings.json and/or .pi/settings.json","description":"# Goal
Implement persistence for settings changes made from `/settings`.

# Required Behavior (legacy)
- Global settings: `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`
- Project overrides: `.pi/settings.json`
- Merge semantics: project overrides global (nested objects merged).

# Deliverables
- Helper to:
  - load existing JSON (if present)
  - update only the intended fields (minimal diffs)
  - write atomically (temp file + rename) with appropriate permissions

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Writes are atomic and do not corrupt settings on crash.
- [ ] Project vs global selection is explicit (UI or default behavior).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-39q8","title":"Phase 2: Custom streamSimple handler for extension providers","description":"Add support for custom streaming handlers in extension-registered providers.

## Background
Phase 1 (bd-1oj9) implements basic provider registration with standard APIs.
This phase adds support for custom streaming via JavaScript callbacks.

## API Addition
```typescript
pi.registerProvider(name, {
  // ... base config
  streamSimple?: (request) => AsyncIterable<string>
});
```

## Implementation Requirements
1. Async callback bridge from Rust to JS
2. Stream chunks from JS handler to agent loop
3. Error handling for JS exceptions during streaming
4. Cancellation support (cancel JS stream on interrupt)

## Technical Challenges
- JS -> Rust async iteration
- Back-pressure handling
- Error propagation across language boundary

## Test Plan
- Unit test: custom streamSimple called for provider
- Integration test: streaming works end-to-end
- Test: cancellation stops JS stream

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (stream bridge)
- src/extensions_js.rs (async callback support)
- src/provider.rs (use extension stream)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:36:36.961318176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:30:01.456330680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:29:25.285844625Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39q8","depends_on_id":"bd-1oj9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:36:59.163581435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":247,"issue_id":"bd-39q8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. All 4 requirements implemented.","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:30:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39u","title":"Unit tests: JS runtime + shims + event loop ordering","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires correct promise/microtask ordering and shim behavior.
- This bead is the **ordering/invariants test suite** for bd-123 + bd-1f5.

Steps:
- Unit/property tests for the PiJS event loop ordering:
  - macrotask vs microtask fixpoint draining
  - timer tie-breaking stability
  - hostcall completion enqueue order stability
- Tests for cancellation + timeout behavior across the promise bridge.
- Trace-log assertions for ordering and promise resolution sequence (diffable).

Acceptance:
- Ordering invariants from bd-123 hold and are reproducible under deterministic harness control.
- These tests protect the promise that the pinned sample set can be run with stable traces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

All 35 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:28:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39za","title":"PiWasm: Tests (unit + conformance + security)","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive coverage for the PiWasm bridge.

# Scope
- Unit tests: compile/instantiate success, invalid wasm, memory grow limits, trap mapping.
- Security tests: denied imports, exceeded budgets, malicious loops (interrupt handler).
- Conformance fixtures: deterministic traces for wasm-in-js scenarios.

# Acceptance
- Tests run in CI and are deterministic.
- Failure diffs include actionable context (imports shape, limits, trap reason).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:31.029810247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:39.624811784Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39za","depends_on_id":"bd-10s6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:39.624767272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39za","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:31.036449110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39za","depends_on_id":"bd-ltk7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:37.872619746Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3am2","title":"Workstream: Error UX (actionable suggestions + context-aware hints)","description":"# Goal
Improve error UX so failures are not just "what happened" but also "what to do next" (actionable suggestions) and "why this happened here" (context-aware hints).

# Background
Pi runs in many environments (different terminals, missing system deps like `fd`, missing API keys, VCR playback mode, etc.). The port already has structured error types, but the user experience depends on:
- concise, readable rendering
- correct categorization
- a small set of high-signal remediation hints

# Principles
- Hints must be **actionable** (specific command/path/setting), not generic.
- Hints must be **non-spammy**: show at most a couple of suggestions per error.
- Hints must be **deterministic** and testable (unit/integration tests assert on messages).

# Candidate Hint Categories (examples)
- Missing API key → point to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / etc and settings override.
- Missing system dependency (`fd`, `rg`) → exact install commands per OS.
- VCR cassette missing in CI → explain `VCR_MODE` + cassette dir + how to record.
- Permission denied reading/writing files → show the path and suggest chmod/ownership checks.
- Invalid config JSON → show file path + first parse error location.
- Provider auth failure (401) → suggest `/login` or `--api-key` usage.

# Deliverables
- A mapping from core error variants → short "next steps" hints.
- Rendering integration for:
  - interactive TUI error panels
  - print mode stderr
  - RPC mode error payloads (where appropriate)
- Tests validating the most important hint paths.

# Non-Goals
- Not building a full diagnostics engine.

 {"id":"bd-3ap","title":"Implement TUI state transition tests","description":"# Implement TUI state transition tests

## Goal
Test the interactive TUI's state machine logic - how it responds to messages
and user input events.

## Background
PiApp in src/interactive.rs implements the bubbletea Model trait:
- init() - Initialize state
- update(msg) - Handle messages, return commands
- view() - Render current state

The update() function is the state machine - it needs thorough testing.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for each test.
- Log initial state, input message, and key state deltas.
- On failure, dump a compact state diff (selected fields) and the rendered view.

## Test Categories

### 1. Keyboard Input Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_escape_quits() {
    let mut app = create_app();
    let cmd = app.update(KeyMsg::Escape);
    assert!(matches!(cmd, Cmd::Quit));
}
```

### 2. Agent Event Tests
- Start/end events toggle streaming
- Text delta accumulation
- Tool call lifecycle updates

### 3. Slash Command Tests
- /help, /model, /thinking, /clear

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Error display and clear-on-input behavior

### 5. Viewport/Scrolling Tests
- Scroll to bottom on new output
- PageUp/PageDown behavior

## Dependencies
- bd-1d3 (Snapshot infrastructure for view testing)

## Files
- tests/tui_state.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 30+ state transition tests
- [ ] All key handlers tested
- [ ] All PiMsg variants tested
- [ ] All slash commands tested
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Scrolling tested
- [ ] Logs include inputs + state deltas
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

 {"id":"bd-cru","title":"Keybindings: define action catalog + default bindings","description":"# Goal
Create the authoritative list of interactive **actions** and their **default keybindings**, matching legacy Pi Agent.

# Background
Legacy action list and defaults are documented in:
- `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/keybindings.md`

This task is the foundation for parsing config overrides and routing key events.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Define an `AppAction`-like enum (or equivalent) with all actions from the legacy docs:
  - Cursor movement
  - Deletion
  - Text input (`newLine`, `submit`, `tab`)
  - Kill ring (`yank`, `yankPop`, `undo`) — if not implemented yet, still define actions for future parity
  - Clipboard (`copy`, `pasteImage`)
  - Application (`interrupt`, `clear`, `exit`, `suspend`, `externalEditor`)
  - Session (`newSession`, `tree`, `fork`)
  - Models & thinking (`selectModel`, `cycleModelForward/backward`, `cycleThinkingLevel`)
  - Display (`expandTools`, `toggleThinking`)
  - Message queue (`followUp`, `dequeue`)
  - Selection lists/pickers
  - Session picker actions

- Define the default bindings for each action (keys list) matching the legacy doc.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep the action IDs stable (strings or serde names) so they round-trip with JSON config.
- Prefer a single source of truth for:
  - action id (machine)
  - display name (human)
  - default keys
  - category (for `/hotkeys` grouping)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Action list matches legacy (no missing actions).
- [ ] Default bindings match legacy defaults.
- [ ] There is a way to iterate actions (for `/hotkeys` rendering).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:36:08.298061995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:18.312113745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:08.670942141Z","close_reason":"Created keybindings.rs with full action catalog (48 actions in 12 categories), default bindings matching legacy, JSON serialization support. Module compiles but tests blocked by unrelated errors in other modules being modified by other agents.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cru","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-d7gn","title":"Feature: Research & selection of popular Pi extensions","description":"Research and select the global 200+ most-used Pi extensions (incl. OpenClaw ecosystem) using an explicit popularity rubric, reproducible discovery queries, and evidence-backed tiering.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:25.249934256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:46:05.914190678Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","research","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","depends_on_id":"bd-po15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:25.276760533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":249,"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Purpose
Define and execute the research + selection methodology for the expanded extension corpus. This is the foundation for all downstream pinning, gap analysis, and conformance work.

Key Questions
- What does “popular” mean in this ecosystem? (stars, forks, usage, mentions, downloads, recency)
- Which sources are authoritative? (pi-mono, GitHub orgs, npm packages, community lists)
- How do we ensure coverage across extension types, complexity, and capability surface?

Outputs
- A documented scoring rubric with explicit thresholds and coverage quotas.
- A candidate inventory list with metadata per extension (repo, commit/tag, license, type, deps, runtime tier, capabilities).
- Tier-1/Tier-2 selection with justification for each chosen extension.

Constraints
- The final corpus must be runnable unmodified; if something breaks, we fix the runtime/shims, not the extension.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:10Z"},{"id":291,"issue_id":"bd-d7gn","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"RESEARCH METHOD (2026-02-05)

Goal
- Discover, validate, and select the global set of “most used” Pi extensions (target: 200+ must-pass) across GitHub, npm, official Pi sources, and OpenClaw/marketplace ecosystems.

Critical definition: “True Pi extension”
A candidate is only considered a Pi extension if it is compatible with Pi’s extension protocol (or can be made compatible via *generic* pipeline transforms):
- Exposes an entrypoint consistent with Pi’s extension API (e.g., default export taking an ExtensionAPI / ctx-like object).
- Uses recognizable Pi extension registration patterns (tools, slash commands, event hooks, provider registration, UI/RPC calls).
- NOT merely “agent prompts”, “Claude Code plugins”, or unrelated MCP servers unless they explicitly implement Pi’s extension protocol.

Evidence-backed popularity (rubric anchor)
- Use bd-29ko rubric as the baseline.
- Extend evidence sources for new ecosystems:
  - Marketplace rank/installs (OpenClaw/ClawHub or analogous)
  - GitHub stars/forks + recent activity
  - npm downloads (weekly) + dependents
  - Cross-repo mentions (README/issue references)

Selection output (what downstream work expects)
- A machine-readable candidate inventory (raw, unfiltered) + a validated/deduped inventory.
- A tiered selection with explicit quotas:
  - Tier-1 >=200 (MUST PASS)
  - Tier-2 additional stretch / long-tail coverage
- Every chosen extension includes:
  - source pin (repo URL + commit/tag + path)
  - license status
  - declared interaction model + capabilities
  - scenario linkage (what conformance tests must do)

Anti-goals / pitfalls
- No one-off, per-extension compatibility hacks.
- No silent exclusions: if something is popular but incompatible, it becomes an explicit gap with an explicit reason.
- Avoid confusing other ecosystems’ “extensions” with Pi extensions; validate via code signatures before counting.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:03:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-dj27","title":"Release: versioning + tag strategy (pre-1.0 → 1.0)","description":"# Goal
Define the single source of truth for versioning and tags so releases are repeatable and humans don’t have to remember tribal knowledge.

# Background
- This repo ships a `pi` binary and also exposes a library crate (`pi`).
- Publishing to crates.io and publishing GitHub Releases both need a consistent version scheme.

# Decisions to Make (write down explicitly)
1) **When do we call it 1.0?**
   - What is the minimum feature set / stability bar?
2) **Version coupling:**
   - Are `pi_agent_rust` crate version, `pi` binary `--version`, and any published sibling crates (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust) versioned together or independently?
3) **Tag format:**
   - e.g. `vX.Y.Z` tags for repo releases.
4) **Changelog source:**
   - Prefer generating `CHANGELOG.md` from closed beads via `br changelog` (see sibling task).

# Output / Deliverables
- A short doc section (can live in `docs/releasing.md`) describing:
  - version bump procedure
  - tag naming
  - how to cut a patch release
  - what is required before tagging (quality gates + CI green)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A human can follow the written steps and create a release without guessing.
- [ ] Version/tag policy is consistent with existing `.github/workflows/publish.yml` and crate metadata.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:20:59.131169345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:19.729663150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:42:50.759173199Z","close_reason":"Added docs/releasing.md with version/tag policy + release steps (commit 99b00e4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dj27","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-doi","title":"Coverage tooling + CI gating (llvm-cov)","description":"Goal:
- Add repeatable coverage tooling (llvm-cov) and CI gating that enforces agreed coverage thresholds and the no-mock policy.

Why this matters:
- Coverage is only meaningful if it reflects real behavior (no mocks) and is stable in CI.
- We need artifacts + logs to debug coverage regressions quickly.

Scope:
- Choose tool (cargo llvm-cov preferred) and pin exact command(s).
- Add local + CI commands for coverage runs (text + optional HTML).
- Define thresholds (overall + per-module if feasible) based on bd-351 output.
- Force VCR playback in CI (no network) and ensure secrets are redacted.
- Emit detailed logging for coverage runs (command, env, duration, artifacts).

Deliverables:
- Documented coverage command in README/CONFORMANCE.md or docs/testing.
- CI step that fails when coverage drops below threshold.
- Coverage artifacts (summary + HTML) and logs stored as CI artifacts.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Running the coverage command locally produces a report without network calls.
- CI fails on threshold breach and passes with current baseline.
- Logs clearly show command, env (redacted), duration, artifact paths, and summary.
- No mocks/fakes are used to raise coverage (enforced by policy check).

Dependencies:
- Uses bd-351 coverage matrix for thresholds/scope.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:14.468787870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:43.048354433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:37:33.106312804Z","close_reason":"Coverage baseline captured; CI gate set to 30%","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-doi","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-doi","depends_on_id":"bd-351","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
